title: The Boys Of Bummer

jokes.text: I got hung up at the snack stand watching those hot dogs rolling and turning, not a care in the world.
jokes.setup: Homer explains why he's late to the baseball game
jokes.punchline: He was mesmerized by hot dogs living their best life
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer character moment - his childlike wonder at mundane things. The personification of hot dogs 'not having a care' is genuinely funny and uniquely Homer.

jokes.text: By my calculations, we're only one strike away from victory. Numbers are fun.
jokes.setup: Martin states the obvious about the game situation
jokes.punchline: His earnest declaration that 'Numbers are fun'
jokes.explanation: Classic Martin nerd humor - his genuine enthusiasm for math is endearing and character-appropriate.

jokes.text: who made me this beautiful black bicep warmer! It keeps me naughty hands busy.
jokes.setup: Willie thanks Groundskeeper Willie for making something
jokes.punchline: It's a bicep warmer that keeps his 'naughty hands' busy
jokes.explanation: Willie's accent and crude humor combined with the absurdity of a 'bicep warmer' for behavioral control is very in character.

jokes.text: I got it! I got it! I hogged it! I hogged it!
jokes.setup: Bart confidently calls for the ball
jokes.punchline: Switches to 'hogged it' - suggesting he selfishly took it
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective wordplay that shows Bart's cockiness even in the moment of success.

jokes.text: This makes up for everything that's gone wrong in my life or ever will!
jokes.setup: Homer celebrates Bart catching the ball
jokes.punchline: Claims it compensates for ALL future problems too
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer hyperbole - the escalation to future problems is perfectly absurd and character-driven.

jokes.text: Behold my naked butt! Each cheek is a god to you!
jokes.setup: Team celebrates victory
jokes.punchline: Nelson's grandiose declaration about his exposed buttocks
jokes.explanation: Peak Nelson - combining crude physicality with mock-religious grandiosity. The 'each cheek is a god' line is memorably ridiculous.

jokes.text: In this modern-day Deadwood you can do anything.
jokes.setup: Kids ask if they can egg the crowd
jokes.punchline: Homer compares Springfield to lawless Deadwood
jokes.explanation: Good cultural reference that frames Springfield's chaos through Homer's perspective.

jokes.text: Well, he's done very well for someone who came out blue and backwards.
jokes.setup: Marge bragging about Bart to store clerk
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of traumatic birth details
jokes.explanation: Classic Simpsons dark humor - Marge's matter-of-fact delivery of disturbing birth information while bragging is perfectly uncomfortable.

jokes.text: Listen, do you mind if I hum while you talk?
jokes.setup: Marge won't stop bragging about Bart
jokes.punchline: Clerk asks permission to tune her out
jokes.explanation: Brutally honest service worker response - the politeness makes it funnier than if they were just rude.

jokes.text: I hate shopping! I'm bored; my feet hurt; I'm too big for the choo-choo train.
jokes.setup: Homer complains about shopping
jokes.punchline: The specific indignity of being too big for the children's train
jokes.explanation: The escalation from general complaints to the specific childish desire denied by his size is very Homer.

jokes.text: Unless you're serving me a cold beer, you keep moving.
jokes.setup: Homer approaches occupied husband chair
jokes.punchline: Comic Book Guy's territorial beer-based ultimatum
jokes.explanation: Good character moment for Comic Book Guy - his conditions for interaction are perfectly antisocial.

jokes.text: My wife's lookin' for a bathing suit that doesn't make her look 'horsey.' So, I'm gonna be here a while.
jokes.setup: Another man explains why he's camping in the husband chair
jokes.punchline: Casually insulting description of his wife's appearance
jokes.explanation: Cletus's casual cruelty toward his wife is character-appropriate but relies on mean-spirited humor.

jokes.text: Sure feels weird lying on top of the covers.
jokes.setup: Homer tests a mattress in the store
jokes.punchline: His observation about the strangeness of the situation
jokes.explanation: Mild observational humor setting up the escalating bed sequence.

jokes.text: Sure feels weird lying in bed with my pants on.
jokes.setup: Homer continues testing the mattress
jokes.punchline: Next level of inappropriateness - pants still on
jokes.explanation: Part of the escalating sequence - moderate escalation but predictable progression.

jokes.text: It works with my body's natural curves and supports my back-boobs.
jokes.setup: Homer praises the mattress quality
jokes.punchline: References his 'back-boobs' as a selling point
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer body humor - 'back-boobs' is a perfectly crude Homer coinage that's both gross and endearing.

jokes.text: That's the best endorsement I've heard since Mickey Mantle for Maypo!
jokes.setup: Manager impressed by Homer's endorsement
jokes.punchline: Obscure vintage cereal commercial reference
jokes.explanation: Deep-cut cultural reference that rewards knowledgeable viewers - Mickey Mantle did do Maypo cereal ads.

jokes.text: You do now, son! You're hired!
jokes.setup: Homer says he doesn't work there
jokes.punchline: Manager immediately hires him based on the testimonial
jokes.explanation: Simple absurdist business logic - hiring someone on the spot for an accidental endorsement.

jokes.text: Why don't thousands come to watch a teacher inspire a child?
jokes.setup: Skinner questions why baseball gets such crowds
jokes.punchline: His earnest belief that teaching deserves equal attention
jokes.explanation: Perfect Skinner - his delusional self-importance and genuine belief in education's entertainment value.

jokes.text: But it did happen. Just yesterday, in Crazytown!
jokes.setup: Responding to Skinner's rhetorical question
jokes.punchline: Sarcastically places inspiring teaching in fictional 'Crazytown'
jokes.explanation: Chalmers delivers perfect deadpan sarcasm - 'Crazytown' is the ideal dismissive response to Skinner's earnestness.

jokes.text: Luigi's, they deliver on flavor, but not to your house!
jokes.setup: Mention of Luigi's pizza party prize
jokes.punchline: Contradictory advertising slogan about delivery
jokes.explanation: Classic Simpsons fake advertising - the contradiction between 'deliver on flavor' and 'not to your house' is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: The bullets will come down! And kill my sister.
jokes.setup: Warning against celebratory gunfire
jokes.punchline: Specific concern about his sister being killed
jokes.explanation: Dark humor delivered matter-of-factly - the personal specificity of the sister makes the physics lesson funnier.

jokes.text: It's like watching a monkey swallow a hand grenade.
jokes.setup: Describing Bart's terrible ball-handling
jokes.punchline: Vivid and disturbing comparison
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark simile - captures both the horrific fascination and inevitable disaster of Bart's fumble. Highly quotable and memorable.

jokes.text: If Homer had used some protection, we'd all be poopin' roses on easy street!
jokes.setup: Crowd blaming Homer for creating Bart
jokes.punchline: Crude suggestion about contraception leading to paradise
jokes.explanation: Peak Moe vulgarity - blaming Bart's existence for everyone's problems while using his characteristic crude imagery.

jokes.text: Was there even a baseball game yesterday? I don't remember...
jokes.setup: Bart says his life is over after the game
jokes.punchline: Marge pretends not to remember the traumatic event
jokes.explanation: Classic protective mother behavior - Marge's attempt to minimize trauma through selective memory is both sweet and futile.

jokes.text: Let me tell you about a land where it rains beer and hails pretzels and you can play swim-up blackjack with Abraham Lincoln.
jokes.setup: Homer's mattress sales pitch
jokes.punchline: Incredibly specific fantasy involving Lincoln gambling
jokes.explanation: Fantastic Homer fantasy - the escalation from beer rain to Lincoln blackjack is perfectly absurd and memorable. The specificity makes it genuinely funny.

jokes.text: I feel like I'm floating on a river of corpses.
jokes.setup: Customer tries Homer's recommended mattress
jokes.punchline: Disturbing but satisfied description of comfort
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark comfort description - the morbid imagery delivered as a positive review is perfectly Simpsons.

jokes.text: I'm a licensed mattress professional, if there were such a thing
jokes.setup: Homer presents his credentials
jokes.punchline: Admits the credential doesn't exist
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer honesty undermining his own authority - the structure of building up then immediately deflating his expertise is well-crafted.

jokes.text: Okay, who else is dead in bed?
jokes.setup: Homer addressing relationship problems
jokes.punchline: Crudely asking about sexual dysfunction
jokes.explanation: Homer's blunt approach to sensitive topics - crude but economical phrasing.

jokes.text: He died, Bart. Dad buried him in the backyard. But not in that order.
jokes.setup: Bart asks about his rabbit that went 'upstate'
jokes.punchline: Lisa reveals death, then adds disturbing clarification about timing
jokes.explanation: Exceptional dark humor - the setup creates expectation of death revelation, but 'not in that order' adds horrifying implication that Homer buried it alive. Perfect structure and timing.

jokes.text: hitting Eleanor Roosevelt, who up till then had been a great beauty.
jokes.setup: LaBoot describes throwing ball into stands
jokes.punchline: Claims he ruined Eleanor Roosevelt's famous looks
jokes.explanation: Brilliant historical revisionism - suggesting Eleanor Roosevelt was beautiful until a baseball injury is absurdist perfection with great historical knowledge required.

jokes.text: Yeah, in Idaho.
jokes.setup: LaBoot brags about his great life after failure
jokes.punchline: Bart's dismissive response to the location
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of LaBoot's attempt at inspiration - Bart's depression makes him dismiss even success if it's in Idaho.

jokes.text: You stink like a Dutchman's throw-up.
jokes.setup: Fan recognizes Bart at card show
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific ethnic insult
jokes.explanation: Weird specificity of 'Dutchman's throw-up' is memorable but not particularly clever - relies on randomness.

jokes.text: I had had like a million Doritos. You know, the ones with the flame on the bag? Oh-ho, that bag ain't lyin'!
jokes.setup: Homer explains drinking holy water
jokes.punchline: Endorses Doritos advertising accuracy
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer product enthusiasm - getting sidetracked from his excuse to praise snack food marketing is very in character.

jokes.text: We tried raising Cain, but we weren't able.
jokes.setup: Lovejoys complain about mattress
jokes.punchline: Biblical pun about sexual problems
jokes.explanation: Excellent biblical wordplay from the reverend - 'raising Cain' works both as troublemaking and sexual euphemism, perfectly character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Pay to the order of Stupid Reverend. Memo: Bite my ass.
jokes.setup: Homer writes a refund check
jokes.punchline: Insults the reverend in the official check fields
jokes.explanation: Peak Homer passive-aggression - using official financial documents for crude insults is perfectly childish.

jokes.text: It was made for Elvis, but he found it tacky.
jokes.setup: Homer describes his mattress history
jokes.punchline: Elvis, king of tacky, found something too tacky
jokes.explanation: Great ironic reversal - if Elvis found something tacky, it must be extraordinarily gaudy. The impossibility makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Lousy Reverend, he's having my sex while I'm at home with Marge not doing anything sensual or erotic.
jokes.setup: Homer realizes the mattress trade consequences
jokes.punchline: Possessive attitude about 'his' sex being stolen
jokes.explanation: Homer's childish possessiveness about sex as a commodity that can be stolen is perfectly immature and uncomfortable.

jokes.text: See? I hate me, too. Now we can be friends again.
jokes.setup: Bart shows his self-vandalized room to Lisa
jokes.punchline: Logic that self-hatred creates friendship
jokes.explanation: Dark but touching logic - Bart's attempt to find common ground through self-loathing is both sad and oddly logical for a kid.

jokes.text: Well, I wrote a best-selling series of mystery novels. Stephen King called it, 'Scary, good fun.'
jokes.setup: Marge asks what townspeople have ever accomplished
jokes.punchline: Agnes has unexpected literary success
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character surprise - Agnes Skinner being a successful horror novelist with Stephen King's endorsement is perfectly unexpected and memorable.

jokes.text: if you pretend to be a ghost at an old amusement park I'm trying to buy.
jokes.setup: Series of increasingly strange favor requests
jokes.punchline: Scooby-Doo parody request
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation to Scooby-Doo territory - the specificity of the fake ghost scheme is absurdly detailed and funny.

jokes.text: It's not like they're made of skunk tails and horse nipples.
jokes.setup: Announcer defending sausage quality
jokes.punchline: Specific gross ingredients that definitely ARE in them
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup/punchline - saying what's NOT in sausage implies that's exactly what IS in them. 'Horse nipples' is memorably specific.

jokes.text: It looks like a grilled-cheese sandwich. Or a chicken club. Or a Reuben. Or a Monte Cristo, or...
jokes.setup: Homer and Marge look at their half-mattress
jokes.punchline: Homer's food-obsessed interpretation continues
jokes.explanation: Predictable Homer food obsession - sees everything as sandwiches. The escalation is character-appropriate but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: We even have the same bum looking at us.
jokes.setup: Homer and Marge making love behind billboard
jokes.punchline: References their honeymoon having identical circumstances
jokes.explanation: Good callback establishing their honeymoon was equally pathetic - the continuity of having the same homeless witness is absurdly romantic.

jokes.text: And the 78th time is the charm!
jokes.setup: Multiple do-overs before Bart finally catches the ball
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous number of attempts needed
jokes.explanation: Good escalation joke - the specific high number of attempts makes the 'fix' absurdly obvious and desperate.

jokes.text: It was a fake just now.
jokes.setup: Milhouse reveals the game was fake
jokes.punchline: Then claims THAT revelation was fake
jokes.explanation: Good meta-humor about truth and deception - the double-fake creates confusion about what's real, mirroring the episode's themes.

tmdb_overview: Bart goes from hero to goat on his Little League team after an error in the championship game. Meanwhile, Homer falls asleep on a bed in a department store. When he's awakened, he raves about the mattress and is given a job as a salesman.
